R Notebook

This is an R Markdown Notebook. When you execute code within the notebook, the results appear beneath the code.

Try executing this chunk by clicking the Run button within the chunk or by placing your cursor inside it and pressing Ctrl+Shift+Enter.

Add a new chunk by clicking the Insert Chunk button on the toolbar or by pressing Ctrl+Alt+I.

When you save the notebook, an HTML file containing the code and output will be saved alongside it (click the Preview button or press Ctrl+Shift+K to preview the HTML file).

The preview shows you a rendered HTML copy of the contents of the editor. Consequently, unlike Knit, Preview does not run any R code chunks. Instead, the output of the chunk when it was last run in the editor is displayed.


# Task 1 a


library(dplyr)
library(tidyr)

# Load the dataset
students <- read.csv("Studentmarks.csv", stringsAsFactors = FALSE)

# Split dob using "/" separator
students <- students %>%
  separate(dob, into = c("date", "month", "year"), sep = "/", convert = TRUE)

# Rename columns
colnames(students) <- c("StudentID", "Studentname", "date", "month", "year", "X2020", "X2021", "X2022")

# Calculate age1 and age2
students <- students %>%
  mutate(
    dob_full = as.Date(paste(year, month, date, sep = "-"), format = "%Y-%m-%d"),
    age1 = as.numeric(difftime(Sys.Date(), dob_full, units = "days")) / 365.25,
    age2 = as.numeric(format(Sys.Date(), "%Y")) - year
  ) %>%
  select(-dob_full)

print(students)



#The code reads the CSV file. 
#It splits the date of birth into day, month, and year. 
#Each part is converted to numbers. 
#It renames the columns for clarity. 
#It creates a full date. Then it calculates two ages. 
#One uses the exact date, the other uses only the year.

# Task 1 b


library(ggplot2)

# Reshape data for plotting
students_long <- students %>%
  pivot_longer(cols = c(X2020, X2021, X2022),
               names_to = "ExamYear", values_to = "Marks")

# Scatter plot with Studentname on y-axis
ggplot(students_long, aes(x = Marks, y = Studentname, color = ExamYear)) +
  geom_point(size = 3) +
  labs(title = "Student Marks by Year", x = "Marks", y = "Studentname") +
  theme_minimal()




#The code changes the data shape for plotting. 
#It puts all yearly marks into one column. 
#It uses ggplot2 to draw a scatter plot. 
#Each point shows a student’s marks. 
#Colours show different years. 
#It helps compare how each student performed across 2020, 2021, and 2022.

# Task 1 c


# Calculate total marks
students_rank <- students %>%
  mutate(Total = X2020 + X2021 + X2022) %>%
  filter(Total >= 200) %>%
  arrange(desc(Total))

# Plot ranking
ggplot(students_rank, aes(x = reorder(Studentname, -Total), y = Total)) +
  geom_col(fill = "steelblue") +
  labs(title = "Total Marks of Students (>=200) - Descending Order",
       x = "Student Name", y = "Total Marks") +
  theme_minimal()


#The code adds the marks from all three years. 
#It filters students who scored 200 or more. 
#Then it arranges them from highest to lowest. 
#It uses ggplot2 to make a bar chart. 
#The chart shows which students performed best based on total marks.

# Task 2 a

library(dplyr)


fireDirty = read.csv("fire_2019_dirty.csv")

totalMissing = sum(is.na(fireDirty))
cat("Total NA values in dataset:", totalMissing, "\n\n")
Total NA values in dataset: 12607 
missingPerColumn = colSums(is.na(fireDirty))
cat("Missing values per column:\n")
Missing values per column:
print(missingPerColumn)
  latitude  longitude brightness       scan      track   acq_date   acq_time  satellite instrument confidence    version bright_t31        frp   daynight       type 
         0          0       1801          0          0          0          0       1801       1801       1801          0       1801       1801       1801          0 
columnsWithMissing = names(missingPerColumn[missingPerColumn > 0])
cat("\nColumns containing NA:\n")

Columns containing NA:
print(columnsWithMissing)
[1] "brightness" "satellite"  "instrument" "confidence" "bright_t31" "frp"        "daynight"  
percentageMissing = (missingPerColumn / nrow(fireDirty)) * 100
cat("\nPercentage missing per column:\n")

Percentage missing per column:
print(round(percentageMissing, 2))
  latitude  longitude brightness       scan      track   acq_date   acq_time  satellite instrument confidence    version bright_t31        frp   daynight       type 
         0          0          5          0          0          0          0          5          5          5          0          5          5          5          0 
brightnessQ1 = quantile(fireDirty$brightness, 0.25, na.rm = TRUE)
brightnessQ3 = quantile(fireDirty$brightness, 0.75, na.rm = TRUE)
brightnessIqr = brightnessQ3 - brightnessQ1
brightnessLowerBound = brightnessQ1 - 1.5 * brightnessIqr
brightnessUpperBound = brightnessQ3 + 1.5 * brightnessIqr
brightnessOutliers = sum(fireDirty$brightness < brightnessLowerBound | 
                         fireDirty$brightness > brightnessUpperBound, 
                         na.rm = TRUE)
cat("Outliers:", brightnessOutliers, "\n")
Outliers: 1400 
confidenceQ1 = quantile(fireDirty$confidence, 0.25, na.rm = TRUE)
confidenceQ3 = quantile(fireDirty$confidence, 0.75, na.rm = TRUE)
confidenceIqr = confidenceQ3 - confidenceQ1
confidenceLowerBound = confidenceQ1 - 1.5 * confidenceIqr
confidenceUpperBound = confidenceQ3 + 1.5 * confidenceIqr
confidenceOutliers = sum(fireDirty$confidence < confidenceLowerBound | 
                         fireDirty$confidence > confidenceUpperBound, 
                         na.rm = TRUE)
cat("Outliers:", confidenceOutliers, "\n")
Outliers: 301 
brightT31Q1 = quantile(fireDirty$bright_t31, 0.25, na.rm = TRUE)
brightT31Q3 = quantile(fireDirty$bright_t31, 0.75, na.rm = TRUE)
brightT31Iqr = brightT31Q3 - brightT31Q1
brightT31LowerBound = brightT31Q1 - 1.5 * brightT31Iqr
brightT31UpperBound = brightT31Q3 + 1.5 * brightT31Iqr
brightT31Outliers = sum(fireDirty$bright_t31 < brightT31LowerBound | 
                        fireDirty$bright_t31 > brightT31UpperBound, 
                        na.rm = TRUE)
cat("Outliers:", brightT31Outliers, "\n")
Outliers: 39 
frpQ1 = quantile(fireDirty$frp, 0.25, na.rm = TRUE)
frpQ3 = quantile(fireDirty$frp, 0.75, na.rm = TRUE)
frpIqr = frpQ3 - frpQ1
frpLowerBound = frpQ1 - 1.5 * frpIqr
frpUpperBound = frpQ3 + 1.5 * frpIqr
frpOutliers = sum(fireDirty$frp < frpLowerBound | 
                  fireDirty$frp > frpUpperBound, 
                  na.rm = TRUE)
cat("Outliers:", frpOutliers, "\n")
Outliers: 3275 
# The fire_2019_dirty.csv dataset contains 12,607 missing values across 7 features. 
# Each feature has exactly 1,801 NAs. This represents 5 percent missing data. 
# Four numerical and three categorical features have missing data.

# Numerical features include brightness, confidence, bright_t31, and frp. 
# I will use local median imputation. The method uses 5 nearby observations. 
# Brightness contains 1,400 outliers. Confidence has 301 outliers. 
# Bright_t31 shows 39 outliers. FRP exhibits 3,275 outliers. 
# Median is robust to outliers. Local median is preferred over global median. 
# Global median may change the distribution. Local median preserves sequential 
# patterns by considering nearby values.

# Categorical features include satellite, instrument, and daynight. 
# I will use local mode imputation. Nearby fire observations share similar patterns. 
# Local mode considers surrounding observations. Global mode uses the entire dataset. 
# Local mode is better for fire monitoring data. Fire detections close together 
# share similar characteristics.

# Task 2 b 

library(dplyr)
library(ggplot2)

fireDirty = read.csv("fire_2019_dirty.csv")
fireClean = read.csv("fire_2019_clean.csv")

cat("Dirty data rows:", nrow(fireDirty), "\n")
Dirty data rows: 36011 
cat("Clean data rows:", nrow(fireClean), "\n\n")
Clean data rows: 36011 
fireModified = fireDirty

brightnessNaRows = which(is.na(fireModified$brightness))
confidenceNaRows = which(is.na(fireModified$confidence))
brightT31NaRows = which(is.na(fireModified$bright_t31))
frpNaRows = which(is.na(fireModified$frp))


cat("Brightness missing rows:", length(brightnessNaRows), "\n")
Brightness missing rows: 1801 
cat("Confidence missing rows:", length(confidenceNaRows), "\n")
Confidence missing rows: 1801 
cat("Bright_t31 missing rows:", length(brightT31NaRows), "\n")
Bright_t31 missing rows: 1801 
cat("FRP missing rows:", length(frpNaRows), "\n\n")
FRP missing rows: 1801 
imputeLocalMedian = function(x, window = 5) {
  halfWindow = floor(window / 2)
  naPositions = which(is.na(x))
  
  for (i in naPositions) {
    start = max(1, i - halfWindow)
    end = min(length(x), i + halfWindow)
    
    nearbyValues = x[start:end]
    nearbyValues = nearbyValues[!is.na(nearbyValues)]
    
    if (length(nearbyValues) > 0) {
      x[i] = median(nearbyValues, na.rm = TRUE)
    }
  }
  
  return(x)
}


fireModified$brightness = imputeLocalMedian(fireModified$brightness, window = 5)


fireModified$confidence = imputeLocalMedian(fireModified$confidence, window = 5)


fireModified$bright_t31 = imputeLocalMedian(fireModified$bright_t31, window = 5)


fireModified$frp = imputeLocalMedian(fireModified$frp, window = 5)


cat("Statistical comparision of brightness\n")
Statistical comparision of brightness
brightnessImputed = fireModified$brightness[brightnessNaRows]
brightnessActual = fireClean$brightness[brightnessNaRows]

brightnessAbsoluteDeviation = abs(brightnessImputed - brightnessActual)
brightnessPercentDeviation = (brightnessAbsoluteDeviation / brightnessActual) * 100

brightnessMeanDeviation = mean(brightnessAbsoluteDeviation)
brightnessMedianDeviation = median(brightnessAbsoluteDeviation)
brightnessMaxDeviation = max(brightnessAbsoluteDeviation)
brightnessMinDeviation = min(brightnessAbsoluteDeviation)
brightnessSdDeviation = sd(brightnessAbsoluteDeviation)

cat("Mean of actual clean values:", round(mean(brightnessActual), 2), "\n")
Mean of actual clean values: 328.69 
cat("Median of actual clean values:", round(median(brightnessActual), 2), "\n\n")
Median of actual clean values: 326.7 
cat("Deviation Statistics:\n")
Deviation Statistics:
cat("  Mean Absolute Deviation:", round(brightnessMeanDeviation, 2), "\n")
  Mean Absolute Deviation: 9.74 
cat("  Median Absolute Deviation:", round(brightnessMedianDeviation, 2), "\n")
  Median Absolute Deviation: 6.15 
cat("  Min Deviation:", round(brightnessMinDeviation, 2), "\n")
  Min Deviation: 0 
cat("  Max Deviation:", round(brightnessMaxDeviation, 2), "\n")
  Max Deviation: 85.65 
cat("  SD of Deviation:", round(brightnessSdDeviation, 2), "\n")
  SD of Deviation: 11.5 
cat("  Mean Percent Deviation:", round(mean(brightnessPercentDeviation), 2), "%\n\n")
  Mean Percent Deviation: 2.87 %
brightnessMeanOriginal = mean(fireClean$brightness)
brightnessMeanModified = mean(fireModified$brightness)
brightnessMedianOriginal = median(fireClean$brightness)
brightnessMedianModified = median(fireModified$brightness)

cat("Overall Dataset Impact:\n")
Overall Dataset Impact:
cat("  Clean Mean:", round(brightnessMeanOriginal, 2), "\n")
  Clean Mean: 328.75 
cat("  Modified Mean:", round(brightnessMeanModified, 2), "\n")
  Modified Mean: 328.67 
cat("  Difference:", round(brightnessMeanModified - brightnessMeanOriginal, 2), "\n")
  Difference: -0.08 
cat("  Clean Median:", round(brightnessMedianOriginal, 2), "\n")
  Clean Median: 326.4 
cat("  Modified Median:", round(brightnessMedianModified, 2), "\n")
  Modified Median: 326.4 
cat("  Difference:", round(brightnessMedianModified - brightnessMedianOriginal, 2), "\n\n")
  Difference: 0 
cat("Statistical comparision of confidence\n")
Statistical comparision of confidence
confidenceImputed = fireModified$confidence[confidenceNaRows]
confidenceActual = fireClean$confidence[confidenceNaRows]

confidenceAbsoluteDeviation = abs(confidenceImputed - confidenceActual)
confidencePercentDeviation = (confidenceAbsoluteDeviation / confidenceActual) * 100

confidenceMeanDeviation = mean(confidenceAbsoluteDeviation)
confidenceMedianDeviation = median(confidenceAbsoluteDeviation)
confidenceMaxDeviation = max(confidenceAbsoluteDeviation)
confidenceMinDeviation = min(confidenceAbsoluteDeviation)
confidenceSdDeviation = sd(confidenceAbsoluteDeviation)

cat("Mean of actual clean values:", round(mean(confidenceActual), 2), "\n")

confidenceMeanModified = mean(fireModified$confidence)
confidenceMedianOriginal = median(fireClean$confidence)
confidenceMedianModified = median(fireModified$confidence)

cat("Overall Dataset Impact:\n")
Overall Dataset Impact:

brightT31MeanDeviation = mean(brightT31AbsoluteDeviation)

brightT31MeanOriginal = mean(fireClean$bright_t31)
brightT31MeanModified = mean(fireModified$bright_t31)
brightT31MedianOriginal = median(fireClean$bright_t31)
brightT31MedianModified = median(fireModified$bright_t31)

cat("Overall Dataset Impact:\n")

cat("Statistical comparision of frp\n")
Statistical comparision of frp
frpImputed = fireModified$frp[frpNaRows]
frpActual = fireClean$frp[frpNaRows]

frpAbsoluteDeviation = abs(frpImputed - frpActual)
frpPercentDeviation = (frpAbsoluteDeviation / frpActual) * 100

frpMeanDeviation = mean(frpAbsoluteDeviation)
frpMedianDeviation = median(frpAbsoluteDeviation)
frpMaxDeviation = max(frpAbsoluteDeviation)

brightnessComparisonDf = data.frame(
  Index = 1:length(brightnessNaRows),
  Imputed = brightnessImputed,
  Actual = brightnessActual,
  Deviation = brightnessAbsoluteDeviation
)

plot1 = ggplot(brightnessComparisonDf, aes(x = Actual, y = Imputed)) +
  geom_point(alpha = 0.5, color = "blue") +
  geom_abline(slope = 1, intercept = 0, color = "red", linetype = "dashed") +
  labs(title = "Brightness: Imputed vs Actual Values",
       x = "Actual Clean Values",
       y = "Imputed Values") +
  theme_minimal()
plot1


plot2 = ggplot(brightnessComparisonDf, aes(x = Deviation)) +
  geom_histogram(bins = 30, fill = "steelblue", color = "white") +
  labs(title = "Brightness: Distribution of Absolute Deviations",
       x = "Absolute Deviation",
       y = "Frequency") +
  theme_minimal()
plot2


brightnessDistDf = data.frame(
  Value = c(fireClean$brightness, fireModified$brightness),
  Dataset = c(rep("Clean", nrow(fireClean)), rep("Modified", nrow(fireModified)))
)

plot3 = ggplot(brightnessDistDf, aes(x = Value, fill = Dataset)) +
  geom_density(alpha = 0.5) +
  labs(title = "Brightness: Distribution Comparison",
       x = "Brightness Value",
       y = "Density") +
  theme_minimal()
plot3



confidenceComparisonDf = data.frame(
  Index = 1:length(confidenceNaRows),
  Imputed = confidenceImputed,
  Actual = confidenceActual,
  Deviation = confidenceAbsoluteDeviation
)

plot4 = ggplot(confidenceComparisonDf, aes(x = Actual, y = Imputed)) +
  geom_point(alpha = 0.5, color = "darkgreen") +
  geom_abline(slope = 1, intercept = 0, color = "red", linetype = "dashed") +
  labs(title = "Confidence: Imputed vs Actual Values",
       x = "Actual Clean Values",
       y = "Imputed Values") +
  theme_minimal()
plot4


plot5 = ggplot(confidenceComparisonDf, aes(x = Deviation)) +
  geom_histogram(bins = 30, fill = "darkgreen", color = "white") +
  labs(title = "Confidence: Distribution of Absolute Deviations",
       x = "Absolute Deviation",
       y = "Frequency") +
  theme_minimal()
plot5


confidenceDistDf = data.frame(
  Value = c(fireClean$confidence, fireModified$confidence),
  Dataset = c(rep("Clean", nrow(fireClean)), rep("Modified", nrow(fireModified)))
)

plot6 = ggplot(confidenceDistDf, aes(x = Value, fill = Dataset)) +
  geom_density(alpha = 0.5) +
  labs(title = "Confidence: Distribution Comparison",
       x = "Confidence Value",
       y = "Density") +
  theme_minimal()
plot6



brightT31ComparisonDf = data.frame(
  Index = 1:length(brightT31NaRows),
  Imputed = brightT31Imputed,
  Actual = brightT31Actual,
  Deviation = brightT31AbsoluteDeviation
)

plot7 = ggplot(brightT31ComparisonDf, aes(x = Actual, y = Imputed)) +
  geom_point(alpha = 0.5, color = "purple") +
  geom_abline(slope = 1, intercept = 0, color = "red", linetype = "dashed") +
  labs(title = "Bright_T31: Imputed vs Actual Values",
       x = "Actual Clean Values",
       y = "Imputed Values") +
  theme_minimal()
plot7


plot8 = ggplot(brightT31ComparisonDf, aes(x = Deviation)) +
  geom_histogram(bins = 30, fill = "purple", color = "white") +
  labs(title = "Bright_T31: Distribution of Absolute Deviations",
       x = "Absolute Deviation",
       y = "Frequency") +
  theme_minimal()
plot8


brightT31DistDf = data.frame(
  Value = c(fireClean$bright_t31, fireModified$bright_t31),
  Dataset = c(rep("Clean", nrow(fireClean)), rep("Modified", nrow(fireModified)))
)

plot9 = ggplot(brightT31DistDf, aes(x = Value, fill = Dataset)) +
  geom_density(alpha = 0.5) +
  labs(title = "Bright_T31: Distribution Comparison",
       x = "Bright_T31 Value",
       y = "Density") +
  theme_minimal()
plot9



frpComparisonDf = data.frame(
  Index = 1:length(frpNaRows),
  Imputed = frpImputed,
  Actual = frpActual,
  Deviation = frpAbsoluteDeviation
)

plot10 = ggplot(frpComparisonDf, aes(x = Actual, y = Imputed)) +
  geom_point(alpha = 0.5, color = "orange") +
  geom_abline(slope = 1, intercept = 0, color = "red", linetype = "dashed") +
  labs(title = "FRP: Imputed vs Actual Values",
       x = "Actual Clean Values",
       y = "Imputed Values") +
  theme_minimal()
plot10


plot11 = ggplot(frpComparisonDf, aes(x = Deviation)) +
  geom_histogram(bins = 30, fill = "orange", color = "white") +
  labs(title = "FRP: Distribution of Absolute Deviations",
       x = "Absolute Deviation",
       y = "Frequency") +
  theme_minimal()
plot11


frpDistDf = data.frame(
  Value = c(fireClean$frp, fireModified$frp),
  Dataset = c(rep("Clean", nrow(fireClean)), rep("Modified", nrow(fireModified)))
)

plot12 = ggplot(frpDistDf, aes(x = Value, fill = Dataset)) +
  geom_density(alpha = 0.5) +
  labs(title = "FRP: Distribution Comparison",
       x = "FRP Value",
       y = "Density") +
  theme_minimal()
plot12



write.csv(fireModified, "fire_2019_modified_numeric.csv", row.names = FALSE)

# The imputation analysis was done for four numerical features. 
# The results showed that the imputation method kept the dataset mostly unchanged. 
# Brightness had a mean percent deviation of 2.87%. 
# The imputed values stayed close to the actual ones but scattered more at higher values. 
# Confidence had the biggest absolute deviations, with a maximum of 88.5. 
# However, the median stayed the same, showing that the overall distribution did not shift. 
# Bright_T31 showed the best performance with only 0.83% mean percent deviation and very little scatter. 
# FRP was the most difficult variable. It had 82.65% mean percent deviation and large outliers up to 1239.2. 
# This was expected since FRP values are usually skewed. 
# Even though each feature behaved differently, the dataset as a whole stayed consistent. 
# All four variables had mean differences of less than 0.54 and no median differences. 
# This means the imputation filled missing values well 


#Task 2 c

library(dplyr)
library(ggplot2)


fireDirty = read.csv("fire_2019_modified_numeric.csv")
fireClean = read.csv("fire_2019_clean.csv")


cat("Dirty data rows:", nrow(fireDirty), "\n")
Dirty data rows: 36011 
cat("Clean data rows:", nrow(fireClean), "\n\n")
Clean data rows: 36011 
fireModified = fireDirty


satelliteNaRows = which(is.na(fireModified$satellite))
instrumentNaRows = which(is.na(fireModified$instrument))
daynightNaRows = which(is.na(fireModified$daynight))

cat("Rows with missing values:- n")
Rows with missing values:- n
cat("Satellite missing rows:", length(satelliteNaRows), "\n")
Satellite missing rows: 1801 
cat("Instrument missing rows:", length(instrumentNaRows), "\n")
Instrument missing rows: 1801 
cat("Daynight missing rows:", length(daynightNaRows), "\n\n")
Daynight missing rows: 1801 
imputeLocalMode = function(x, window = 5) {
  halfWindow = floor(window / 2)
  naPositions = which(is.na(x))
  
  for (i in naPositions) {
    start = max(1, i - halfWindow)
    end = min(length(x), i + halfWindow)
    
    nearbyValues = x[start:end]
    nearbyValues = nearbyValues[!is.na(nearbyValues)]
    
    if (length(nearbyValues) > 0) {
      valueFrequency = table(nearbyValues)
      modeValue = names(sort(valueFrequency, decreasing = TRUE))[1]
      x[i] = modeValue
    }
  }
  
  return(x)
}

fireModified$satellite = imputeLocalMode(fireModified$satellite, window = 5)


fireModified$instrument = imputeLocalMode(fireModified$instrument, window = 5)


fireModified$daynight = imputeLocalMode(fireModified$daynight, window = 5)


cat("Statistical comparision of satellite\n")
Statistical comparision of satellite
satelliteImputed = fireModified$satellite[satelliteNaRows]
satelliteActual = fireClean$satellite[satelliteNaRows]


satelliteCorrect = sum(satelliteImputed == satelliteActual)
satelliteIncorrect = sum(satelliteImputed != satelliteActual)
satelliteAccuracy = (satelliteCorrect / length(satelliteImputed)) * 100

cat("Total imputed values:", length(satelliteImputed), "\n")
Total imputed values: 1801 
cat("Correct predictions:", satelliteCorrect, "\n")
Correct predictions: 1787 
cat("Incorrect predictions:", satelliteIncorrect, "\n")
Incorrect predictions: 14 
cat("Accuracy:", round(satelliteAccuracy, 2), "%\n\n")
Accuracy: 99.22 %
cat("Frequency of imputed values:\n")
Frequency of imputed values:
satelliteImputedFreq = table(satelliteImputed)
print(satelliteImputedFreq)
satelliteImputed
 Aqua Terra 
 1084   717 
cat("\nFrequency of actual values:\n")

Frequency of actual values:
satelliteActualFreq = table(satelliteActual)
print(satelliteActualFreq)
satelliteActual
 Aqua Terra 
 1072   729 
cat("\nOverall Dataset Impact:\n")

Overall Dataset Impact:
satelliteCleanFreq = table(fireClean$satellite)
satelliteModifiedFreq = table(fireModified$satellite)

cat("Clean dataset frequencies:\n")
Clean dataset frequencies:
print(satelliteCleanFreq)

 Aqua Terra 
20541 15470 
cat("\nModified dataset frequencies:\n")

Modified dataset frequencies:
print(satelliteModifiedFreq)

 Aqua Terra 
20553 15458 
satelliteCleanProp = prop.table(satelliteCleanFreq) * 100
satelliteModifiedProp = prop.table(satelliteModifiedFreq) * 100

cat("\nClean dataset proportions (%):\n")

Clean dataset proportions (%):
print(round(satelliteCleanProp, 2))

 Aqua Terra 
57.04 42.96 
cat("\nModified dataset proportions (%):\n")

Modified dataset proportions (%):
print(round(satelliteModifiedProp, 2))

 Aqua Terra 
57.07 42.93 
cat("\n")
cat("Statistical comparision of instrument\n")
Statistical comparision of instrument
instrumentImputed = fireModified$instrument[instrumentNaRows]
instrumentActual = fireClean$instrument[instrumentNaRows]


instrumentCorrect = sum(instrumentImputed == instrumentActual)
instrumentIncorrect = sum(instrumentImputed != instrumentActual)
instrumentAccuracy = (instrumentCorrect / length(instrumentImputed)) * 100

cat("Total imputed values:", length(instrumentImputed), "\n")
Total imputed values: 1801 
cat("Correct predictions:", instrumentCorrect, "\n")
Correct predictions: 1801 
cat("Incorrect predictions:", instrumentIncorrect, "\n")
Incorrect predictions: 0 
cat("Accuracy:", round(instrumentAccuracy, 2), "%\n\n")

instrumentImputedFreq = table(instrumentImputed)
print(instrumentImputedFreq)

daynightActual = fireClean$daynight[daynightNaRows]


daynightCorrect = sum(daynightImputed == daynightActual)
daynightIncorrect = sum(daynightImputed != daynightActual)
daynightAccuracy = (daynightCorrect / length(daynightImputed)) * 100

cat("Total imputed values:", length(daynightImputed), "\n")
Total imputed values: 1801 
cat("Correct predictions:", daynightCorrect, "\n")
Correct predictions: 1794 
cat("Incorrect predictions:", daynightIncorrect, "\n")

cat("Accuracy:", round(daynightAccuracy, 2), "%\n\n")

print(daynightImputedFreq)

cat("\n")
satelliteConfusion = data.frame(
  Actual = satelliteActual,
  Imputed = satelliteImputed
)


satelliteFreqComparison = data.frame(
  Category = rep(names(satelliteActualFreq), 2),
  Count = c(as.numeric(satelliteActualFreq), as.numeric(satelliteImputedFreq)),
  Type = rep(c("Actual", "Imputed"), each = length(satelliteActualFreq))
)

plot1 = ggplot(satelliteFreqComparison, aes(x = Category, y = Count, fill = Type)) +
  geom_bar(stat = "identity", position = "dodge") +
  labs(title = "Satellite: Frequency Comparison (Imputed Rows Only)",
       x = "Satellite Type",
       y = "Count",
       fill = "Data Type") +
  theme_minimal()
print(plot1)



satelliteOverallComparison = data.frame(
  Category = rep(names(satelliteCleanFreq), 2),
  Count = c(as.numeric(satelliteCleanFreq), as.numeric(satelliteModifiedFreq)),
  Dataset = rep(c("Clean", "Modified"), each = length(satelliteCleanFreq))
)

plot2 = ggplot(satelliteOverallComparison, aes(x = Category, y = Count, fill = Dataset)) +
  geom_bar(stat = "identity", position = "dodge") +
  labs(title = "Satellite: Overall Dataset Distribution",
       x = "Satellite Type",
       y = "Count",
       fill = "Dataset") +
  theme_minimal()
print(plot2)



satelliteAccuracyDf = data.frame(
  Result = c("Correct", "Incorrect"),
  Count = c(satelliteCorrect, satelliteIncorrect)
)

plot3 = ggplot(satelliteAccuracyDf, aes(x = "", y = Count, fill = Result)) +
  geom_bar(stat = "identity", width = 1) +
  labs(title = paste("Satellite Imputation Accuracy:", round(satelliteAccuracy, 2), "%"),
       x = "",
       y = "Count") +
  theme_minimal()
print(plot3)



instrumentFreqComparison = data.frame(
  Category = rep(names(instrumentActualFreq), 2),
  Count = c(as.numeric(instrumentActualFreq), as.numeric(instrumentImputedFreq)),
  Type = rep(c("Actual", "Imputed"), each = length(instrumentActualFreq))
)

plot4 = ggplot(instrumentFreqComparison, aes(x = Category, y = Count, fill = Type)) +
  geom_bar(stat = "identity", position = "dodge") +
  labs(title = "Instrument: Frequency Comparison (Imputed Rows Only)",
       x = "Instrument Type",
       y = "Count",
       fill = "Data Type") +
  theme_minimal()
print(plot4)



instrumentOverallComparison = data.frame(
  Category = rep(names(instrumentCleanFreq), 2),
  Count = c(as.numeric(instrumentCleanFreq), as.numeric(instrumentModifiedFreq)),
  Dataset = rep(c("Clean", "Modified"), each = length(instrumentCleanFreq))
)

plot5 = ggplot(instrumentOverallComparison, aes(x = Category, y = Count, fill = Dataset)) +
  geom_bar(stat = "identity", position = "dodge") +
  labs(title = "Instrument: Overall Dataset Distribution",
       x = "Instrument Type",
       y = "Count",
       fill = "Dataset") +
  theme_minimal()
print(plot5)



instrumentAccuracyDf = data.frame(
  Result = c("Correct", "Incorrect"),
  Count = c(instrumentCorrect, instrumentIncorrect)
)

plot6 = ggplot(instrumentAccuracyDf, aes(x = "", y = Count, fill = Result)) +
  geom_bar(stat = "identity", width = 1) +
  labs(title = paste("Instrument Imputation Accuracy:", round(instrumentAccuracy, 2), "%"),
       x = "",
       y = "Count") +
  theme_minimal()
print(plot6)



daynightFreqComparison = data.frame(
  Category = rep(names(daynightActualFreq), 2),
  Count = c(as.numeric(daynightActualFreq), as.numeric(daynightImputedFreq)),
  Type = rep(c("Actual", "Imputed"), each = length(daynightActualFreq))
)

plot7 = ggplot(daynightFreqComparison, aes(x = Category, y = Count, fill = Type)) +
  geom_bar(stat = "identity", position = "dodge") +
  labs(title = "Daynight: Frequency Comparison (Imputed Rows Only)",
       x = "Day/Night",
       y = "Count",
       fill = "Data Type") +
  theme_minimal()
print(plot7)



daynightOverallComparison = data.frame(
  Category = rep(names(daynightCleanFreq), 2),
  Count = c(as.numeric(daynightCleanFreq), as.numeric(daynightModifiedFreq)),
  Dataset = rep(c("Clean", "Modified"), each = length(daynightCleanFreq))
)

plot8 = ggplot(daynightOverallComparison, aes(x = Category, y = Count, fill = Dataset)) +
  geom_bar(stat = "identity", position = "dodge") +
  labs(title = "Daynight: Overall Dataset Distribution",
       x = "Day/Night",
       y = "Count",
       fill = "Dataset") +
  theme_minimal()
print(plot8)



daynightAccuracyDf = data.frame(
  Result = c("Correct", "Incorrect"),
  Count = c(daynightCorrect, daynightIncorrect)
)

plot9 = ggplot(daynightAccuracyDf, aes(x = "", y = Count, fill = Result)) +
  geom_bar(stat = "identity", width = 1) +
  labs(title = paste("Daynight Imputation Accuracy:", round(daynightAccuracy, 2), "%"),
       x = "",
       y = "Count") +
  theme_minimal()
print(plot9)



overallAccuracyDf = data.frame(
  Feature = c("Satellite", "Instrument", "Daynight"),
  Accuracy = c(satelliteAccuracy, instrumentAccuracy, daynightAccuracy)
)

plot10 = ggplot(overallAccuracyDf, aes(x = Feature, y = Accuracy, fill = Feature)) +
  geom_bar(stat = "identity") +
  labs(title = "Overall Imputation Accuracy for Categorical Features",
       x = "Feature",
       y = "Accuracy (%)") +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(legend.position = "none")
print(plot10)



write.csv(fireModified, "fire_2019_modified.csv", row.names = FALSE)

# The local mode imputation method filled missing values for three categorical 
# features with very high accuracy. For satellite, it reached 99.22 percent accuracy. 
# It used a five-row window around each missing value and chose the most common category. 
# For instrument, accuracy was 100 percent because all records used MODIS data. 
# For day and night, accuracy was 99.61 percent, showing that it worked well even though 
# the data was not balanced with 78 percent day and 22 percent night. 
# The overall dataset stayed almost the same. The satellite proportion changed by only 
# 0.03 percent and day and night by 0.01 percent. This shows that the imputation kept 
# the original structure. The window-based method worked well because it used nearby data, 
# which makes sense for fire detection data where nearby records often share similar 
# satellite and time patterns.


# Task 2 d

cleanData = read.csv("fire_2019_clean.csv")
modifiedData = read.csv("fire_2019_modified.csv")

xFeature = "brightness"
yFeature = "confidence"


ggplot(cleanData, aes_string(x = xFeature, y = yFeature)) +
  geom_point(color = "blue", alpha = 0.4) +
  geom_smooth(method = "lm", color = "darkblue", se = FALSE) +
  labs(title = "Bivariate Analysis (Clean Data)",
       x = xFeature,
       y = yFeature) +
  theme_minimal()



ggplot(modifiedData, aes_string(x = xFeature, y = yFeature)) +
  geom_point(color = "red", alpha = 0.4) +
  geom_smooth(method = "lm", color = "darkred", se = FALSE) +
  labs(title = "Bivariate Analysis (Modified Data)",
       x = xFeature,
       y = yFeature) +
  theme_minimal()


# The bivariate analysis of brightness and confidence shows how both variables relate 
# in the clean and modified datasets. In the clean data, most values are grouped in the 
# lower brightness range between 300 and 350, with confidence spread between 0 and 100. 
# There is a weak positive trend between them. The modified data shows the same pattern 
# and distribution, though it looks denser in the imputed areas. Both datasets share the 
# same relationship. Confidence levels reach around 100 when brightness goes above 350, 
# while lower brightness values show more variation. This pattern makes sense because 
# brighter fires are detected with higher and more stable confidence. The regression lines 
# in both plots follow the same path, showing that imputation did not change the relationship. 
# The modified data kept the original pattern intact.
